---
title: "Create and manage vendor purchase orders in TokenSupply"
description: "Walk through the full PO lifecycle — from draft to closed — including tax calculations, stock collection, invoice review, and PDF export."
sidebarTitle: "Purchase Orders"
---

Purchase Orders are how you procure stock from vendors. Each PO tracks the full procurement lifecycle — from the moment you raise the order through to payment confirmation and stock collection — in a single, auditable record. Navigate to **Purchases → Purchase Orders** to manage them.

## Creating a purchase order

Click **New Purchase Order** from the list view. The form is organised into cards:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Select a vendor">
    Search for and select an active vendor. The vendor's badge shows whether they are **Individual** or **Business**. Selecting a vendor automatically pre-fills the **Currency** and **Payment Terms** from their profile.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Select a product">
    Pick the product you are procuring. If a vendor offer exists for this vendor–product combination, the **Unit Price** and **Delivery Time** are auto-filled from the offer.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set quantity, price, and currency">
    Enter the **Quantity** and confirm or override the **Unit Price**. Currency defaults to the vendor's default currency but can be changed per PO.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Configure tax">
    Tax is calculated automatically based on vendor type and geography:

    - **Individual vendors** — always 0% tax, no override. The PDF prints a Tax Declaration notice.
    - **Business vendors** — the rate and label are derived from your store country vs the vendor's country. Examples: **GST 18%** for an Indian vendor selling to an Indian store, **0%** intra-EU reverse charge for EU-to-EU transactions.

    Toggle **Tax Inclusive** if the unit price you entered already includes tax — TokenSupply will back-calculate and display the breakdown correctly.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add cost adjustments">
    Optionally add discounts or additional charges as labelled line items — for example, shipping fees, handling charges, or FX conversion costs. Each adjustment requires a label and an amount.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Set terms and delivery">
    Fill in **Payment Terms**, **Due Date**, **Delivery Method** (Email by default), a **Vendor Note** (visible to the vendor), and up to **8 tags** for internal categorisation.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Attach an invoice (optional)">
    You can attach a vendor invoice at creation time. The file lands in **Pending Review** immediately and follows the standard invoice approval workflow.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save or confirm">
    Click **Save as Draft** to keep editing, or **Confirm** to send the PO to the vendor. Confirming triggers an email notification to the vendor and your admins.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  Use **Duplicate** from the More Actions menu on any existing PO to open the creation form pre-filled with the same vendor, product, quantity, unit price, payment terms, and tax settings. This is the fastest way to raise a repeat order.
</Tip>

## PO numbering

PO numbers are generated automatically using the format `PO-{VENDOR_CODE}-{SEQUENCE}`. For example, the 42nd PO raised for vendor `SLR-001` becomes `PO-SLR-001-0042`. Sequences are scoped per vendor, per store — so each vendor has their own counter starting from 1.

## PO lifecycle

A purchase order moves through five statuses, in order:

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **Draft** | Saved but not yet sent to the vendor. You can freely edit the PO. |
| **Confirmed** | Shared with the vendor and awaiting payment. Vendor can now upload stock and invoices. |
| **Payment Pending** | Payment is in progress. |
| **Paid** | Payment has been confirmed. |
| **Closed** | Procurement is complete and the PO is locked. No further edits are permitted. |

Use the **Mark as [Next Status]** button to advance the PO. To revert one step, use the **Undo** action. Each transition is timestamped and triggers a status-change email to the vendor and your admins.

<Info>
  Stock collection runs in parallel with the lifecycle — it is not a separate status. A vendor can begin submitting stock as soon as the PO is Confirmed, regardless of whether payment has been marked.
</Info>

## Collecting stock

Once a PO is **Confirmed**, the stock collection workflow becomes available. Stock can be submitted by the vendor through their portal or by an admin directly on the PO.

To add stock, open the **Inventory** section on the PO detail page and click **Add Stock**:

- **Paste keys** — enter one key per line in the text area.
- **Account products** — use the format `username | password` per line.
- **Bulk import** — upload a CSV or TXT file to load keys in bulk.
- **Expiry date** — optionally apply a single expiry date to all items in the batch.
- **Duplicate detection** — the system checks each key against existing inventory in real time. Duplicates are flagged inline with a warning marker before submission.
- **Quantity cap** — you cannot submit more items than the remaining quantity on the PO.

When the submitted count reaches the PO quantity, TokenSupply marks the stock collection as complete and all items appear under the linked product's **Inventory** tab, ready for sale or fulfilment.

If you need to correct a stock submission, use **Reset Stock** to clear all linked inventory items and start over.

## Invoice workflow

Vendors (or admins) can upload an invoice file to a PO once it is **Confirmed** or later:

1. Upload the invoice file — the status changes to **Pending Review**.
2. An admin opens the **Review** dialog and either **Approves** or **Rejects** the invoice, with optional notes.
3. The decision and any reviewer notes are stored on the PO and surfaced in the activity timeline.

## More actions

The **More Actions** menu on a PO detail page offers:

| Action | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Edit** | Update PO details (available until Closed). |
| **Duplicate** | Pre-fills a new PO with the same settings. |
| **Download PDF** | Exports the PO as a formatted PDF. |
| **Delete** | Permanently deletes the PO — requires typing `DELETE` to confirm. Linked tickets are unlinked and PO notes are removed. |

## PO PDF

The exported PDF is generated client-side and includes:

- Both parties' addresses and tax IDs
- Line items with quantity, unit price, and totals
- Tax breakdown (or Tax Declaration block for Individual vendors)
- Grand total
- Vendor bank details (IFSC, SWIFT/BIC, IBAN, UPI, PayPal — whichever fields are populated on the vendor profile)

## PO notes

The **PO Notes** card on the detail page is a team-only internal thread. Vendor users cannot see notes, even if they have portal access. Each note displays the author's name and a colour-coded role badge (Admin or Support).

This is separate from the **Vendor Note** field set during PO creation, which is visible to the vendor on their portal.

## Due date reminders

A daily background job checks all open POs against their due dates. Email and in-app notifications are sent to the relevant team members when a PO is approaching or past its due date.
